news.title:
Nifty Metal index gained 7.6% in February; Tata Steel, Adani Ent., Jindal Steel, Lloyds advanced 10-13%.
Brokerages favor JSW Steel, Vedanta, Lloyds with strong demand, price hikes, and growth prospects driving optimism.
news.summary:
Nifty Metal index gained 7.6% in February; Tata Steel, Adani Ent., Jindal Steel, Lloyds advanced 10-13%.
Brokerages favor JSW Steel, Vedanta, Lloyds with strong demand, price hikes, and growth prospects driving optimism.

news.title:
Skyhigh Sustainable acquired 4.97 cr shares (4.87%) of Jindal Steel Ltd from Gagan Infraenergy Ltd.
Acquisition was an off-market transfer under SEBI regulations, maintaining total voting capital unchanged.
news.summary:
Skyhigh Sustainable acquired 4.97 cr shares (4.87%) of Jindal Steel Ltd from Gagan Infraenergy Ltd.
Acquisition was an off-market transfer under SEBI regulations, maintaining total voting capital unchanged.

news.title:
Skyhigh Sustainable Ltd plans to acquire 4.87% of Jindal Steel Ltd shares via off-market transfer.
Acquisition aims to consolidate Jindal Steel Ltd's shareholding within the promoter group entities.
news.summary:
Skyhigh Sustainable Ltd plans to acquire 4.87% of Jindal Steel Ltd shares via off-market transfer.
Acquisition aims to consolidate Jindal Steel Ltd's shareholding within the promoter group entities.

news.title:
EU steel tariffs improve Thyssenkrupp's outlook amid Jindal stake sale talks.
Thyssenkrupp CEO sees positive sentiment, aiding steel division sale to Jindal.
news.summary:
EU steel tariffs improve Thyssenkrupp's outlook amid Jindal stake sale talks.
Thyssenkrupp CEO sees positive sentiment, aiding steel division sale to Jindal.

news.title:
Jindal Steel shifts focus to flat products, targeting 55% flat and 45% long ratio by Q4 FY26.
The company aims for 70% value-added product profile as capacities expand.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel shifts focus to flat products, targeting 55% flat and 45% long ratio by Q4 FY26.
The company aims for 70% value-added product profile as capacities expand.

news.title:
Jindal Steel plans to commission Basic Oxygen Furnace-III at Angul by Q4 FY26, boosting production capacity to 15.6 MT.
Slurry pipeline completion by FY26 end may save ₹750-850/tonne in costs.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel plans to commission Basic Oxygen Furnace-III at Angul by Q4 FY26, boosting production capacity to 15.6 MT.
Slurry pipeline completion by FY26 end may save ₹750-850/tonne in costs.

news.title:
Jindal Steel forecasts coking coal costs to rise by $18-$20/tonne in Q4 FY26.
Domestic steel prices increased by INR 3,000-3,500/tonne since Dec 2025; strong demand expected to sustain.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel forecasts coking coal costs to rise by $18-$20/tonne in Q4 FY26.
Domestic steel prices increased by INR 3,000-3,500/tonne since Dec 2025; strong demand expected to sustain.

news.title: Citi retains a bearish stance on Jindal Steel, cutting target to 785.
news.summary: Citi retains a bearish stance on Jindal Steel, cutting target to 785.

news.title:
Jindal Steel reports Q3 net profit of ₹188.5 Cr, revenue at ₹13,026 Cr.
EBITDA stands at ₹1,632 Cr with a margin of 12.5%.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel reports Q3 net profit of ₹188.5 Cr, revenue at ₹13,026 Cr.
EBITDA stands at ₹1,632 Cr with a margin of 12.5%.

news.title:
Nomura maintains 'Buy' on Tata Steel, JSW Steel, Jindal Steel citing strong domestic price momentum despite global challenges.
HRC prices rose 11% QoQ to Rs 52,400/tonne due to safeguard duties, cost pressures, and improved market sentiment.
news.summary:
Nomura maintains 'Buy' on Tata Steel, JSW Steel, Jindal Steel citing strong domestic price momentum despite global challenges.
HRC prices rose 11% QoQ to Rs 52,400/tonne due to safeguard duties, cost pressures, and improved market sentiment.

news.title:
Morgan Stanley upgrades JSPL to Overweight from Equal Weight.
Target price for JSPL raised to Rs 1250 by Morgan Stanley.
news.summary:
Morgan Stanley upgrades JSPL to Overweight from Equal Weight.
Target price for JSPL raised to Rs 1250 by Morgan Stanley.

news.title: Jindal Steel to review Q3 earnings on January 30.
news.summary: Jindal Steel to review Q3 earnings on January 30.

news.title:
Thyssenkrupp considers gradually selling its steel unit TKSE to Jindal Steel.
This move highlights strategic changes within the steel sector.
news.summary:
Thyssenkrupp considers gradually selling its steel unit TKSE to Jindal Steel.
This move highlights strategic changes within the steel sector.

news.title:
Indian firms like ONGC Videsh, IOC, and Reliance face geopolitical risks due to Venezuela turmoil, impacting oil projects and investments.
Pharma firms like Sun Pharma, Glenmark, and Cipla, along with Jindal Steel's mining ties, face operational and financial uncertainties.
news.summary:
Indian firms like ONGC Videsh, IOC, and Reliance face geopolitical risks due to Venezuela turmoil, impacting oil projects and investments.
Pharma firms like Sun Pharma, Glenmark, and Cipla, along with Jindal Steel's mining ties, face operational and financial uncertainties.

news.title:
Govt imposes 3-year safeguard duty on select steel imports to curb cheap inflows, supporting domestic prices and earnings.
Brokerages see potential re-rating for steelmakers like JSW Steel, SAIL, Tata Steel, and Jindal Steel, but downstream impact raises concerns.
news.summary:
Govt imposes 3-year safeguard duty on select steel imports to curb cheap inflows, supporting domestic prices and earnings.
Brokerages see potential re-rating for steelmakers like JSW Steel, SAIL, Tata Steel, and Jindal Steel, but downstream impact raises concerns.

news.title:
Steel stocks surged as India imposed anti-dumping duty. JSW Steel rose 5%, Tata Steel 2.5%, SAIL 4.2%, and Jindal Steel 3.3%.
The duty starts at 12% in year one, reducing to 11% by year three. Imports from China, Vietnam, Nepal are affected; specialty steel products excluded.
news.summary:
Steel stocks surged as India imposed anti-dumping duty. JSW Steel rose 5%, Tata Steel 2.5%, SAIL 4.2%, and Jindal Steel 3.3%.
The duty starts at 12% in year one, reducing to 11% by year three. Imports from China, Vietnam, Nepal are affected; specialty steel products excluded.

news.title:
Jindal Steel faces GST audit alleging excess ITC for FY 21-23.
Demand order of Rs 21.5 crore, Rs 2.1 crore penalty issued; company may appeal.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel faces GST audit alleging excess ITC for FY 21-23.
Demand order of Rs 21.5 crore, Rs 2.1 crore penalty issued; company may appeal.

news.title:
Jindal Steel's Q3FY26 outlook shows muted performance due to lower realisations and higher CoP.
Volumes may rise, but coking coal costs are expected to increase by ₹3-7/tonne in Q3.
news.summary:
Jindal Steel's Q3FY26 outlook shows muted performance due to lower realisations and higher CoP.
Volumes may rise, but coking coal costs are expected to increase by ₹3-7/tonne in Q3.

news.title:
Nuvama cuts Jindal Steel's price target by 9.7% to ₹1,264, still implying a 24.8% upside.
JSPL's EBITDA CAGR forecast is 28% for FY25-28, despite near-term steel price, cost challenges.
news.summary:
Nuvama cuts Jindal Steel's price target by 9.7% to ₹1,264, still implying a 24.8% upside.
JSPL's EBITDA CAGR forecast is 28% for FY25-28, despite near-term steel price, cost challenges.

news.title:
IndiGo, Jindal Steel, and Zydus Lifesciences topped five-year December returns, while Kirloskar Brothers and Thomas Cook lagged.
Nifty Midcap 100 and Smallcap 100 outperformed Nifty 50 in December; analysts forecast FY26-27 earnings growth of 10-16% YoY.
news.summary:
IndiGo, Jindal Steel, and Zydus Lifesciences topped five-year December returns, while Kirloskar Brothers and Thomas Cook lagged.
Nifty Midcap 100 and Smallcap 100 outperformed Nifty 50 in December; analysts forecast FY26-27 earnings growth of 10-16% YoY.
